stats.fact: 88% of MCP servers require credentials, but only 8.5% use OAuth — the majority still rely on static API keys or PATs
53% of MCP servers rely on static API keys or Personal Access Tokens (PATs), which are long-lived and rarely rotated
79% of API keys in MCP servers are passed via simple environment variables
492 publicly exposed MCP servers were found lacking basic authentication or encryption
86% of enterprise MCP servers have implemented RFC 8707-compliant resource indicators as of 2026
9,400 published MCP servers across four major registries as of Q2 2026, growing at +58% QoQ
MCP adoption is accelerating faster than security controls, with 2026 focused on closing the governance gap
